summary: Recording quality not discussed, but the show is noted for a three‑guitar interplay with Crosby’s clean rhythm work on “The Other One”.
review: Both reviewers focus more on the vibe than technical fidelity. Phil_McGee praises the unusual three‑guitar texture on “The Other One” (and another tune), highlighting Crosby’s cleaner, thinner tone compared to Jerry and Bob. The other reviewer offers no concrete comments about sound or performance, leaving the recording quality indeterminate.

ai_rating.rationale: Only two reviews, one with a neutral/poetic comment and another positive about guitar interplay; no information on audio fidelity, leading to an average overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers did not mention sound clarity, mix, or source type, so recording quality cannot be assessed definitively.

show_quality.setlist_flow: No specific comments on pacing or set structure were provided.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Compared to Crosby’s clean tone, Jerry’s guitar is described as thicker/heavier.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s rhythm work is mentioned only in contrast to Crosby’s cleaner sound.
